prompt:
Declaration (in builder format):

DistributedRalph
  .pool(6 implementer on "sonnet-4.6", 1 reviewer on "opus-4.7", max 2 per repo)
  .backlog("fix_plan.md" in "github.com/lambda-run/ops")
      .claim(branch lock, 10m ttl)
      .release(on merge, abandon, or ttl expiry)
  .protocol
      .conflict(last write wins after rebase)
      .escalate(3 rebase conflicts to reviewer pool)
      .gossip("slack:#ralph-bus" on claim, merge, block)
  .loop(while backlog has open tasks)
      .stop_if(budget > $50, kill switch, error rate > 30%)
  .on_crash(release claims, requeue with retry++, max 3 then park for human)
  .observe(claim, commit, fail to "loki")
      .metrics(success rate, p50 task seconds, claim contention)
      .surface(error rate > 20% or queue stuck > 30m)
  .terminate(backlog empty or budget exhausted or human signals done)

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
• Agent pool contains 6 implementer agents
• Implementer agents run on model "sonnet-4.6"
• Agent pool contains 1 reviewer agent
• Reviewer agent runs on model "opus-4.7"
• Maximum 2 agents allowed per repository simultaneously
• Backlog source file is "fix_plan.md"
• Backlog file is located in repository "github.com/lambda-run/ops"
• Each task claim acquires a branch lock
• Branch lock TTL is 10 minutes
• Branch lock is released on merge
• Branch lock is released on task abandon
• Branch lock is released on TTL expiry
• Conflict resolution strategy is last-write-wins after rebase
• 3 rebase conflicts on a single task triggers escalation to the reviewer pool
• Gossip notifications are sent to Slack channel "#ralph-bus"
• Gossip event fired on task claim
• Gossip event fired on task merge
• Gossip event fired on task block
• Main execution loop runs while backlog contains open tasks
• Loop stops if cumulative budget exceeds $50
• Loop stops if kill switch is activated
• Loop stops if error rate exceeds 30%
• On agent crash, all held claims are released
• On agent crash, task is requeued with retry counter incremented
• Maximum retry count before parking is 3
• Tasks exceeding 3 retries are parked for human review
• Observability events emitted to Loki
• Observed event types: claim, commit, fail
• Tracked metric: success rate
• Tracked metric: p50 task duration in seconds
• Tracked metric: claim contention
• Alert fires when error rate exceeds 20%
• Alert fires when queue is stuck for more than 30 minutes
• System terminates when backlog is empty
• System terminates when budget is exhausted
• System terminates when a human signals done
